what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard document that declares an intent to perform authoritative web research on Anthropic's official Channels feature (research preview) for MCP Channels and Claude Code, and to summarize the mechanism, contract, delivery constraints, reply path, safety, and build implications for a fork.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, title, author_session, channel=dragnet-development, and an ordered list of six board:// items.
The body is a single short paragraph that states the research topic and scope but contains no actual findings.
History shows the item was filed and then an order list was added moments later.
It has no links or thread value and is authored by session ch-3mpkjg3r.
It references related noticeboard items via board:// URIs in the order field.
standouts: Explicitly frames the topic as 'authoritative web research' on Anthropic's official Channels feature as the 'inject mechanism'.
Tightly scopes the intended output to contract, delivery constraints, reply path, safety, and implications for 'our build' and 'the fork'.
Serves as a hub by ordering six related board items, implying a sequence or thread of research artifacts.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter and a Markdown body.
Uses board:// URIs to reference related items.
Includes created/updated timestamps and a state transition history.
Breaks the convention of substantive content by being a stub in draft state.
concerns: The body is only a scope declaration, not the promised authoritative research.
Claims such as 'official mechanism' and 'authoritative' are not substantiated within the file.
The order list references six other items without any local explanation of their roles.
notes: A draft noticeboard stub that announces a research task on Anthropic's official Channels feature and links to a sequence of related board items.
questions: What are the contents of the six referenced board items?
What is 'the fork' and which build decisions will this research inform?
What does 'inject mechanism' specifically refer to in this context?
Where will the actual research findings be recorded?
What is the role of the dragnet-development channel in this work?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to deliver authoritative web research on MCP Channels and Claude Code, but the actual content is only a brief declaration of intent and scope with no findings, so it does not yet fulfill that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a well-sourced reference for how Anthropic's Channels feature works and how its constraints should shape the project's fork.
novelty: It specifically scopes the research around Anthropic's official 'Channels' research-preview feature as the 'inject mechanism' and ties it directly to a fork's build decisions, rather than treating MCP channels generically.
contribution: It contributes a placeholder hub item that declares a research scope and links to a sequence of related board items, which a fused system could keep as a breadcrumb to the intended analysis.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard document titled 'MCP Channels + Claude Code — web research (official mechanism)' that states an intent to research Anthropic's official Channels feature and lists six related board items in order.
intention_for_embedding: To produce an authoritative reference on Anthropic's Channels feature contract, constraints, reply path, and safety for informing a fork's build.
